title: Treat 'Pre-Season Testing' As A Different Domain
summary: Official F1 guidance says testing laps are confounded by reliability checks, sensors, fuel load, engine mode, track evolution, and non-race objectives.
rationale: Official F1 guidance says testing laps are confounded by reliability checks, sensors, fuel load, engine mode, track evolution, and non-race objectives. Since your data explicitly includes 'Pre-Season Testing', mixing it into the same validation and model behavior as grands prix is a plausible hidden CV trap.
implementation_hint: Run three ablations: exclude testing rows entirely; keep them with strong downweighting; or train a small testing-only expert and blend with the race model using the Race field. Validation should report Year-Race grouped CV with and without testing races so you can see whether current gains are coming from an easier but mismatched subdomain.
expected_effect: Can improve leaderboard transfer if local CV is currently inflated by learning test-session artifacts instead of race strategy.
risk: If test data contains the same testing domain in meaningful volume, removing it could hurt; this needs explicit ablation rather than assumption.
